title: Automating Enterprise Data Matching Workflows @ Yext

blocks.text: Built and scaled a data matching automation workflow at Yext to reduce manual enterprise onboarding work, improve data accuracy, and accelerate client activation across publisher networks. The solution saved 20,000+ work hours annually, eliminated approximately $1M in operating costs, and maintained 96% CSAT.
Problem

Enterprise client onboarding required teams to manually match large datasets across publishers such as Google, Yelp, Tripadvisor, and Yahoo. The process was slow, error-prone, and difficult to scale as customer data volume grew.
Teams manually reviewed thousands of data rows to identify accurate matches.
Data discrepancies created delays in client activation and platform syncing.
Manual verification increased operational cost and introduced human error.
The onboarding process lacked a scalable workflow for repeatable data validation.
My Role

As Platform Product Manager, I identified the operational bottleneck, built an initial automation MVP, partnered with Engineering and Data teams to improve accuracy, and helped scale the workflow across enterprise onboarding use cases.
Solution
Developed an automation workflow that improved data matching and validation across publisher datasets, reducing manual review and creating a repeatable process for enterprise client onboarding.
Automated matching logic for large datasets
Data validation workflows for enterprise onboarding
Exception handling for records requiring manual review
Repeatable workflow patterns across multiple industries and publisher networks
Improved reporting around match quality and operational efficiency
Execution / Product Decisions
Identified a high-volume manual workflow consuming significant operational time during enterprise onboarding.
Learned Python scripting to prototype an automation MVP and validate feasibility quickly.
Built an initial tool to validate healthcare professional data where accuracy and compliance were especially important.
Partnered with Engineering and Data teams to refine matching logic, improve accuracy, and scale the workflow.
Expanded the automation pattern across additional industries and enterprise clients.
Worked with Sales and client-facing teams to ensure the workflow supported onboarding timelines and client expectations.
Standardized the process to reduce manual review while preserving escalation paths for uncertain matches.
Tradeoffs & Product Decisions
Automation vs accuracy: Automated high-confidence matches while preserving manual review for ambiguous cases.
Speed vs trust: Prioritized match quality and exception handling so teams trusted the output enough to use it.
Prototype vs scale: Used a Python MVP to prove value quickly, then partnered with technical teams to improve durability and scalability.
Industry-specific needs vs repeatable platform: Started with healthcare provider data, then generalized the workflow for broader enterprise use cases.
Results & Impact
Saved 20,000+ work hours annually by automating manual data matching workflows.
Reduced operating costs by approximately $1M annually through lower manual review effort.
Maintained 96% CSAT while improving onboarding efficiency.
Accelerated enterprise client activation by reducing data validation bottlenecks.
Improved data accuracy and platform reliability across digital directories and publisher networks.
Reduced manual intervention by standardizing high-confidence automated matching and exception handling.
Metrics Tracked
Work hours saved
Operating cost reduction
Match accuracy
Manual review rate
Enterprise onboarding cycle time
Client activation speed
CSAT
Key Learnings
The best automation opportunities often come from repetitive operational workflows with clear decision rules and high volume.
Prototyping with lightweight technical tools can create enough proof to earn engineering investment.
Automation only scales when teams trust the output, understand the exceptions, and have a clear path for manual review.
Strong platform work turns one-off operational pain into repeatable infrastructure that improves speed, cost, and quality.
Visual Diagrams
Before vs After Data Matching — Before: manual row review → manual verification → delayed activation. After: automated high-confidence matching → exception review → faster sync.
Automation Decision Flow — Input dataset → matching logic → confidence score → auto-match or manual review → publisher sync.
Scaling Pattern — Healthcare provider matching → broader enterprise datasets → repeatable onboarding workflow.
